steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The list is 15, 14, 18, 17, $x$. The sum of the known values is $15 + 14 + 18 + 17 = 64$, so mean $= \frac{64 + x}{5}$. The median is the 3rd value when all five numbers are in order. Test each choice to see which makes mean $\neq$ median.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x = 6$: Ordered: 6, 14, 15, 17, 18. Median $= 15$. Mean $= \frac{70}{5} = 14$. Mean $\neq$ median, so $x = 6$ is NOT a possible value. ✓
  • Choice B — $x = 11$: Ordered: 11, 14, 15, 17, 18. Median $= 15$. Mean $= \frac{75}{5} = 15$. Equal — possible. ✗
  • Choice C — $x = 16$: Ordered: 14, 15, 16, 17, 18. Median $= 16$. Mean $= \frac{80}{5} = 16$. Equal — possible. ✗
  • Choice D — $x = 21$: Ordered: 14, 15, 17, 18, 21. Median $= 17$. Mean $= \frac{85}{5} = 17$. Equal — possible. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
